title: Episode 14
wikitext:
[]: Episode 14             Quote Post by Phaze » Wed Jul 14, 2004 10:05 pm Yes, that episode, in my opinion one of the most complex, hard to understand episodes in the series. Several intruiging points are brought up, including Rei's poem (which I was able to interpret most of), the nature of the attacking angel (we're not even sure what it looks like) the test taking place (were those the Evas without their armor? If they were, they would have free will wouldn't they?), and Asuka's comment "Everything feels fuzzy, the only thing I can feel clearly is my right arm," I also felt, was a good discussion point. Some of these I have found explanations to, I still thought this would be worth a thread.

[]:             Quote Post by Nephilim » Wed Jul 14, 2004 10:42 pm You are mixing episodes 13 and 14. Episode 13 is when they did the tests naked inside the body simulator, and Asuka said that she feels her right arm clearly, is because the body simulator only have the right arm. Episode 14 is when they recapitulate the previous episodes, when Rei says her poem, and when the eva exchange synch tests are done, and when eva 00 went berserker. The mysterous thing about this episode, is why the Eva00 went berserker, and the mysterious images inside Shini's mind.

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Wed Jul 14, 2004 10:48 pm I knew I got something wrong there. Oh well, lets say episodes 13 and 14.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinseki » Wed Jul 14, 2004 10:50 pm Episode 14 reminds me of when most critics came to the assumption that Gainax was running out of money for the show. The recap, the poem, and Unit-00 going beserk again with Shinji was a plausible form for an episode in my opinion. Of course, Gainax could've used less of the same cells in the 'new' footage, but it can't be helped.

[]:             Quote Post by houtaru » Wed Jul 14, 2004 10:57 pm Episodes 14 and up is where the series got really good in my opinion. Much more psychological. Episode 14 may have looked like a 'clip show' but it was this episode when I noticed eva was not your average anime.

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Thu Jul 15, 2004 12:36 am i thought the simulation bodies had both arms, as seen when Rei's body was invaded by the angel...

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Thu Jul 15, 2004 1:51 am houtaru wrote: Episodes 14 and up is where the series got really good in my opinion. Much more psychological. Episode 14 may have looked like a 'clip show' but it was this episode when I noticed eva was not your average anime.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Jul 20, 2004 6:27 pm Episode 14 was in no way indicitive of a lack of money, many shows do a clip episode mid-way through the series around episode 13 or 14. Excel Saga had one, Trigun had one, Nadesico had one, .hack//SIGN had one, etc. Its a common convention in the animation industry to do a clip/recap episode mid-way through the series so that late comers can be brought up to speed somewhat, while saving a bit of money. The difference is that with Eva's clip episode, they were intelligent and worked it into the plot of the show.

[]:             Quote Post by max3000 » Tue Jul 20, 2004 6:50 pm Why would GAINAX have problems with money if Eva was one of the most popular anime in it's time anyway?

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Tue Jul 20, 2004 7:33 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Excel Saga had one, Trigun had one, Nadesico had one, .hack//SIGN had one, etc.

[]:             Quote Post by MongolSquad » Tue Jul 20, 2004 7:59 pm max3000 wrote: Why would GAINAX have problems with money if Eva was one of the most popular anime in it's time anyway?

[]:             Quote Post by max3000 » Wed Jul 21, 2004 5:37 am Ohhh. *Hits himself*

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sat Jun 12, 2010 8:14 pm Ah, good, there's already a topic where I can post this. Why did Gendo lie about the 11th Angel to the Committee? From the script it's obvious that the "reason" is that he doesn't want them thinking that "the schedule" is not going according to their own plans... But the fact that it's so obvious makes me wonder why he did it. You'd expect he'd be better than that; Gendo never looked more suspicious and duplicitous than he did in that scene. Why would he bother to deny the Angel's existence and make himself look so shady?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Jun 12, 2010 9:12 pm You know what I love about Ep. 14? It essentially confirms that NGE has a very distinct two-half structure. It essentially summarizes this by saying "the first half was like this", and then with Rei's monologue and the 00 Berserk it's saying "the second half is going to be like this".

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Jun 13, 2010 1:59 pm Monk Ed wrote: Ah, good, there's already a topic where I can post this. Why did Gendo lie about the 11th Angel to the Committee? From the script it's obvious that the "reason" is that he doesn't want them thinking that "the schedule" is not going according to their own plans... But the fact that it's so obvious makes me wonder why he did it. You'd expect he'd be better than that; Gendo never looked more suspicious and duplicitous than he did in that scene. Why would he bother to deny the Angel's existence and make himself look so shady?

[]: Because he's Gendo, that's why.             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Sun Jun 13, 2010 3:31 pm AuraTwilight wrote: That Angel totally managed to hack NERV and almost destroyed it from the inside out. I wouldn't want to tell my boss of such a negligent fuckup either.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sun Jun 13, 2010 4:13 pm But I thought Gendo got found out anyway? Furthermore, their primary concern seems to be the schedule/scenario. The first thing they mention is that it "happened too early" and that an invasion within Nerv HQ was "outside of our schedule". As if to drive home the point further, at the end of the conversation Gendo assures them that "The time schedule is advancing as described in the Dead Sea Scrolls" and they warn him that "there is no need for you to create a new script". He even repeats, "I realize that. Everything is proceeding according to Seele's script." That makes no fewer than five mentions of the script/schedule/scenario/timeline/whatever. It's the timing of the Angel's appearance, first and foremost, that seems to have them all riled. What's more mysterious is their accusation that the timing is Gendo's fault, as if he had some kind of (indirect?) control over when and where the Angels appear. Gendo's lie seemed to be aimed at covering up the possibility that he was enacting a 'scenario' of his own design. Except that Ireul's existence got found out anyway, so wouldn't that make his lie even more suspicious? He'd have been better off apologizing like he had simply screwed up. A cover-up naturally makes people think of conspiracy; an apology, mere incompetence. ... Edit: NEVERMIND. NEVERMIND. It just occurred to me that by lying, Gendo could later say, after it was found out that an invasion had taken place, that he wasn't there for it and had no idea about it because his subordinates had dealt with it entirely on their own. He could say that those below him claimed it never happened and he was just relaying that to the Committee. The truth of who lied about the invasion taking place would then be dispersed throughout the bureaucratic hierarchy, with all sides blaming each other for the cover-up and there'd be no way to prove who knew what when. Seele would eventually have to throw up their hands and decide it wasn't an issue worth pursuing. That genius....

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Sun Jun 13, 2010 5:06 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: It essentially summarizes this by saying "the first half was like this", and then with Rei's monologue and the 00 Berserk it's saying "the second half is going to be like this".

[]: Re: Episode 14             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sun Jun 13, 2010 6:01 pm As an interesting aside, production materials suggest that episode #14 was in fact originally supposed to be episode #13, but was moved one ahead for some reason. Phaze wrote: Several intruiging points are brought up, including Rei's poem (which I was able to interpret most of)....

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Jun 13, 2010 7:02 pm Didn't Zuggy already offer the definitive take on Rei's monologue?

Several intruiging points are brought up, including Rei's poem (which I was able to interpret most of), the nature of the attacking angel (we're not even sure what it looks like) the test taking place (were those the Evas without their armo
